objective: Onboard the portfolio project by refreshing its source index and populating its .company/memory project profile and module setup with evidence-backed facts, changing no product code.
acceptanceCriteria: The company-map index is fresh at the current target commit, verified by scripts/company-map status portfolio.

.company/memory/project-profile.md records the verified stack, runtime, package manager, key commands, and high-level architecture, each with concrete evidence (file path/line, package.json entry, or index data).

.company/memory/modules.md records one concise verified section per real module (app router, site/avatar components, lib, data, realtime-session API) using the template fields, each fact backed by evidence.
Security-sensitive boundaries (the app/api/realtime-session route and .env.local usage) are flagged in memory without exposing any secret value.
Every recorded fact is evidence-backed; nothing is invented or assumed. Unverifiable items are omitted or explicitly marked as hypothesis.
No product code or any tracked product file is modified; pre-existing dirty files (README.md, untracked AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) are preserved untouched.
constraints: Subscription-only execution (authType subscription); no API keys or paid inference.
Do not modify product code or any tracked file outside .company/memory/ and .company/tasks/.
Do not fix, stage, or revert the pre-existing dirty files README.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md in the target.
Never store secrets, credentials, raw auth output, or machine-absolute paths in .company/.
Stack is TypeScript + Next.js 14 App Router; follow the target's existing conventions.
outOfScope: Any product code or feature change.
Running deployments or executing a full next build.
Adding tests to the product (the project currently defines no test runner).

projectContext.query: project profile stack modules architecture entrypoints commands app components lib data

notes: MEDIUM audit/onboarding task with crisp user-provided acceptance criteria and no product-behavior design; product-manager mini-spec deliberately skipped as ceremony (Boss right-sizing, Constitution rule 7). Index refreshed by the Boss via scripts/company-map build portfolio before delegation.
